features.title: Build automatic workflow generation MVP
features.user_visible_behavior: Users provide product docs, repo/runtime context, optional MCP tools, and service clone/twin declarations; Gauntlet generates high-quality runnable workflows with coverage reports, egress policy, seed data requirements, rubrics, and success conditions.
verification: Given fixture docs and declared service twins, generation returns a product surface map, service map, coverage report, and workflow drafts.
Generated workflows include concrete success conditions, service routing, seed data requirements, and no fake IDs.
Validation rejects workflows with undeclared live egress, unknown services, placeholder IDs, or vague success criteria.
Coverage selection returns distinct workflows across product surfaces, interfaces, services, and difficulty levels.
evidence: Implemented gauntlet/workflows/schema.py, probe.py, service_map.py, graph.py, mcp_probe.py, rules_planner.py, llm_planner.py, harness.py, generate.py, validate.py, and select.py.
Added POST /workflows/generate with typed request validation and empty-input UX error.
Added optional OpenAI-compatible LLM planner with candidate generation and repair pass, keeping deterministic validation and harness scoring as hard gates.
Added Gauntlet native dry-run scorer to reject workflows that are not concrete, interface-targeted, evidence-rich, routed, seeded, and judgeable.
Added product-specific documented-chain fallback workflows for sessions, direct scrape, profiles/auth context, files, and agent traces.

Added tests/test_workflow_generation.py covering multi-service twin generation, MCP tool discovery, unsafe workflow rejection, record-mode mutation rejection, API response shape, malformed request rejection, egress policy safety, fake LLM planning, LLM repair, and workflow target-doc quality regression.

Verified with python -m py_compile gauntlet/*.py gauntlet/workflows/*.py, python -m unittest tests.test_workflow_generation, python gauntlet/build_resolver.py, and python -m json.tool feature_list.json.
features.notes: See docs/agent-context/workflow-generation-mvp-plan.md. This is implemented as a hybrid high-quality generator: rules fallback plus optional LLM planner/repair, deterministic validation, harness dry-run scoring, and coverage selection. Future work should connect these contracts to actual sandbox execution and persistence/review UI.

features.title: Define and implement first real run contract
features.user_visible_behavior: A queued job runs a declared customer workflow instead of only checking whether a MicroVM endpoint returns a non-500 status.
verification: POST /api/sandboxes/[id]/runs creates run in Supabase, resolves SHA+installation_id, dispatches to backend trigger endpoint.
POST /api/runs/[id]/result callback endpoint receives runner outcome and updates Supabase run row.
Old backend /api/sandbox/trigger accepts task_prompt, twins, workflow_id, callback_url, callback_secret.
End-to-end: triggering a run from the UI queues it, the runner executes, and the result is reflected in the UI.
evidence: Extended /Users/aryanjain/projects/Gauntlet/gauntlet/server/routes/sandbox_runner.py ui_trigger to accept all runner.Job workflow params.
Updated web/src/app/api/sandboxes/[id]/runs/route.ts to fetch installation_id, resolve SHA, and dispatch to backend after creating the Supabase run row.
Added web/src/app/api/runs/[id]/result/route.ts callback endpoint that verifies SANDBOX_CALLBACK_SECRET and updates sandbox_runs in Supabase.
Updated branches route to pass through commit_sha from backend.
npm run lint: passed. npm run build: passed. Python AST syntax check: passed.
features.notes: Backend dispatch and callback wiring are in place. End-to-end execution requires: (1) deploy old backend changes to Fly.io, (2) set SANDBOX_CALLBACK_SECRET + NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL in both envs, (3) MicroVM infrastructure (S3 bucket, Lambda) for actual code execution. Graceful degradation: if dispatch fails, run is marked error immediately. If callback URL is unreachable (local dev), run stays queued.

features.title: Add explicit egress routing policy model
features.user_visible_behavior: Each run declares allowed domains and twin/live/record routing modes before sandbox execution starts.
verification: Validate good and bad policy fixtures.
Confirm undeclared domains fail closed.
Confirm policy is included in run artifact output.
features.notes: The architecture doc treats default-deny egress as a core safety property.

features.title: Harden GitHub webhook and Checks integration
features.user_visible_behavior: Push and PR events are verified, debounced, checked out at the exact commit, and reported back with clear status.
verification: Unit-test signature verification, branch delete skip, PR action skip, and job creation.
Mock GitHub API calls for installation token, tarball download, create check, and complete check.
Confirm stale run cancellation completes the old check as cancelled.
features.notes: Existing code has the core shape but needs tests and production queue decisions.

features.title: Design first twin registry and seed-data contract
features.user_visible_behavior: A workflow can request a versioned mock service with per-run seed data and deterministic stateful behavior.
verification: Document service/version/data boundaries.
Implement a minimal local twin fixture, likely SQLite-backed.
Run a POST then GET flow and validate state persists within a run.
features.notes: Start with one service shape before generalizing.

features.title: Audit old Gauntlet repo for selective carry-over
features.user_visible_behavior: Engineering can choose which old modules to port, rewrite, or discard with rationale.
verification: Create a migration matrix with module, value, dependencies, risks, and decision.
Run tests in old repo for any candidate before porting.
Record accepted carry-over in docs.
features.notes: High-value candidates include personas, workflow schema/synthesis, Layer 3 evidence/reporting, FastAPI repositories, Slack route ideas, and frontend design system.
